steps.title: Extract the slope of line k
steps.content: Line k is \(y = 3x - 2\), which is in slope-intercept form \(y = mx + b\). The slope is \(m = 3\). Since line j is parallel to line k, it must have the same slope: \(m = 3\). Line j has the form \(y = 3x + b\).

steps.title: Find the y-intercept and test all choices
steps.content:
Line j passes through \((0, 1)\). Substituting: \(1 = 3(0) + b \implies b = 1\). So line j is \(y = 3x + 1\).
  • Choice A — \(y = x - 2\): Slope = 1 \( eq 3\). Not parallel to line k. ✗
  • Choice B — \(y = 3x + 1\): Slope = 3 (parallel); passes through \((0,1)\) since \(y = 3(0)+1 = 1\). ✓
  • Choice C — \(y = -x - 2\): Slope = \(-1 eq 3\). Not parallel. ✗
  • Choice D — \(y = -3x + 1\): Slope = \(-3 eq 3\). Not parallel (perpendicular slope would be \(- rac{1}{3}\)). ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
